description: Damaged rack upright — bent, dented, or deformed vertical column compromising structural capacity
Missing beam locks — beam-to-upright safety clips absent, allowing beams to dislodge under impact
Overloaded rack bay — visible sagging of beams or pallets exceeding posted load capacity
Missing load capacity signage — rack system lacking posted maximum load per level and total bay capacity
Missing column protectors — rack uprights at aisle ends without bollards or guards to absorb forklift impact
Pallet overhanging rack edge — pallet or load extending beyond beam face creating strike or fall hazard
Forklift traveling with raised forks — forks elevated above 4 inches while moving, reducing stability and visibility
Unsecured forklift load — load not tilted back or too tall/wide, at risk of sliding or falling during transport
Pedestrian in forklift zone — worker on foot inside designated forklift travel lane without separation or escort
Forklift missing overhead guard — operator compartment lacks overhead protection against falling objects
Forklift missing warning light — blue spot or strobe light absent, reducing pedestrian awareness of approaching truck
Dock door open without trailer — overhead door raised with no trailer sealed to the dock, creating fall hazard
Missing dock plate — gap between trailer bed and dock floor without bridge plate, creating trip/fall risk
Trailer not chocked — docked trailer without wheel chocks in place, risking trailer creep during loading
Dock edge without safety striping — dock perimeter lacking yellow/black hazard markings to warn of drop-off
Fire extinguisher missing — required extinguisher absent from mounting location or not visible in designated area
Blocked fire exit — emergency exit door obstructed by pallets, inventory, equipment, or debris
Obstructed sprinkler heads — storage stacked within 18 inches of sprinkler deflectors violating NFPA 13 clearance
Missing exit sign — emergency exit lacking illuminated or photoluminescent exit signage
Aisle obstruction — travel aisle partially or fully blocked by pallets, equipment, trash, or staged goods
Faded floor markings — pedestrian lanes, forklift paths, or staging zones with worn or missing paint lines
Floor spill — liquid, oil, or granular material on floor creating slip hazard without cleanup or warning signs
Tripping hazard — hoses, cables, shrink wrap, strapping, or small debris in walking or driving paths
Worker without hard hat — employee in rack loading zone or under elevated work without head protection
Worker without safety vest — employee in forklift traffic area lacking high-visibility garment
Blocked electrical panel — switchgear or breaker panel obstructed within 36-inch clearance zone (OSHA 1910.303)
Missing SDS or chemical label — hazardous material container without safety data sheet access or proper labeling
Leaking drum — chemical, oil, or waste drum with visible drip, stain, or pooling beneath container
